changes.summary: Free plan (5,000 queries/month) replaced with metered billing at $5/1,000 requests. $5 monthly credit provided as offset (~1,000 queries). Credit cards now actively charged with no spending cap
changes.previous_state: Free plan: 5,000 queries/month, 3 QPS, no credit card required
changes.current_state: Metered billing at $5/1,000 requests with $5 monthly credit (~1,000 free queries). No free plan. Credit card required, no spending cap

changes.summary: Free tier replaced with pay-per-use + $10 one-time credit
changes.previous_state: Free basic endpoints (Like, Follow, List, etc.)
changes.current_state: Pay-per-use pricing, $10 one-time credit for existing users, free plan expired Feb 13

changes.summary: Premium subscription now required for development mode, test users cut from 25 to 5
changes.previous_state: Free developer access to Web API, 25 test users
changes.current_state: Spotify Premium subscription required for dev mode, 5 test users max, multiple endpoints deprecated

changes.summary: Free access ended after 10+ years, now $1,400/year + per-call fees starting April 30
changes.previous_state: Free API access for 10+ years
changes.current_state: $1,400/year subscription + per-call fees (effective April 30, 2026)

changes.summary: $5 Hacker tier eliminated, features merged into free and higher tiers
changes.previous_state: $5/mo Hacker tier with custom domains and more storage
changes.current_state: Free tier and $10/mo+ paid tiers only, Hacker tier removed

changes.summary: Google quietly slashed Gemini API free tier rate limits by 50-80% in late 2025. Gemini 2.5 Flash went from ~250 requests/day to 20-50. Pro model free tier removed entirely. A Google PM admitted generous limits were only supposed to be available for a single weekend
changes.previous_state: Pro model available on free tier, Flash ~250 RPD, Flash-Lite generous limits
changes.current_state: Pro free tier removed. Flash reduced to 10 RPM (~20-50 RPD). Flash-Lite 15 RPM. 50-80% reduction across the board. Gemini 2.0 Flash retiring March 2026

changes.summary: Restructured to credit-based pricing; sites pause on credit exhaustion
changes.previous_state: Traditional tiered pricing with fixed monthly allowances
changes.current_state: 300 credits/month free, sites pause on exhaustion

changes.summary: Every repo committer is now charged as a full Pro seat ($19/mo) when using Netlify CMS or Identity with a private repo. Previously only users who logged into the Netlify dashboard counted as seats
changes.previous_state: Only Netlify dashboard users counted as billable seats
changes.current_state: All repo committers to connected private repos count as Pro seats ($19/mo each) when using CMS or Identity features

changes.summary: Project pause policy tightened — inactive projects now pause after 1 week
changes.previous_state: Longer inactivity window before project pause
changes.current_state: Projects pause after 1 week of inactivity on free tier

changes.summary: Queues added to Workers free plan — message queuing now free
changes.previous_state: Queues was paid-only
changes.current_state: Queues included in free Workers plan

changes.summary: Self-hosted runner per-minute fee ($0.002/min) announced for March 2026 was postponed indefinitely after community backlash. Self-hosted runners remain free. Separately, hosted runner prices were reduced up to 39% (Jan 2026)
changes.previous_state: Self-hosted runners free. Previous per-minute pricing for hosted runners
changes.current_state: Self-hosted runners remain free — no orchestration fees. Hosted runners 39% cheaper. GitHub re-evaluating self-hosted pricing approach

changes.summary: Launched free tier for MCP Web Server — 5,000 requests/month for agent developers
changes.previous_state: Paid-only web scraping API
changes.current_state: 5,000 free monthly requests on Rapid mode, includes search_engine and scrape_as_markdown tools, handles JS rendering and CAPTCHAs

changes.summary: Managed Payments (Merchant of Record) service entering general availability — Stripe handles tax, billing, fraud, and disputes
changes.previous_state: Payment processing only — developers handled tax compliance, fraud, and disputes separately
changes.current_state: Managed Payments acts as Merchant of Record in 75+ countries, handling sales tax/VAT/GST, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, and transaction-level support

changes.summary: Free plan now single-user only. Team collaboration (shared workspaces, collection sharing) requires paid plan at $19/user/month
changes.previous_state: Free plan supported up to 3 users in shared workspaces with collection sharing and 25 monitoring calls/month
changes.current_state: Free plan = single user only with unlimited collections, environments, mock servers, basic monitoring. Teams require Team plan ($19/user/mo) or higher

changes.summary: Forge platform moved from free to consumption-based billing for compute and storage
changes.previous_state: Entirely free — no charges for compute, storage, or KV operations
changes.current_state: Free monthly allowances per app (100K GB-sec compute, 0.1 GB KVS, 1 GB logs, 1 hr SQL), usage above thresholds billed

changes.summary: Volume snapshots now billed — previously free automatic snapshots now cost $0.08/GB-month
changes.previous_state: Automatic volume snapshots included at no cost, enabled by default
changes.current_state: $0.08/GB-month for snapshot storage, first 10 GB free. Snapshots can be disabled to avoid charges

changes.summary: SQLite storage billing began for Durable Objects — previously free during beta
changes.previous_state: SQLite storage in Durable Objects was free during public beta (compute already billed)
changes.current_state: Storage charged per GB-hour on Workers Paid plan, first 10 GB included. Free plan users unaffected

changes.summary: Switched from performance units to spans-based metrics for error and performance monitoring
changes.previous_state: Performance units pricing model
changes.current_state: Spans-based metrics: Developer plan includes 50K errors + 10M spans/month free. Reserved volume pricing replaces on-demand

changes.summary: Moved to fully usage-based pricing model post-Databricks acquisition — free tier storage now per-project, projects increased 10→100, branches capped at 10 per project, Neon Auth added
changes.previous_state: Free tier: 0.5 GB total storage, 191.9 compute hours, 10 projects, unlimited branches
changes.current_state: Free tier: 0.5 GB per project (up to 5 GB across 100 projects), 100 CU-hours/month per project, 10 branches/project, Neon Auth 60K MAU, auto-scaling up to 2 CU, scale-to-zero

changes.summary: Pro plan moved to credit-based model ($20/mo credit pool), Hobby plan metrics renamed and restructured — bandwidth→Fast Data Transfer, serverless split into Active CPU + Provisioned Memory, Blob Storage and Image Transformations added
changes.previous_state: Hobby: 100 GB bandwidth, 100 GB-hours serverless. Pro: fixed per-resource allocations
changes.current_state: Hobby: 100 GB/mo Fast Data Transfer, 4 hrs Active CPU, 360 GB-hrs Provisioned Memory, 1M edge requests, 1 GB Blob Storage, 5K image transformations. Pro: $20/mo credit pool across all resources, SAML SSO and HIPAA included

changes.summary: Data Center pricing increased 15-40% across Jira, Confluence, and JSM. New DC licenses end March 30 — cloud mandatory for new customers
changes.previous_state: Standard Data Center pricing with Advantaged/legacy discounts
changes.current_state: 15% standard list increase, 18-40% for legacy Advantaged pricing tiers. No new DC licenses after March 30, 2026. Full DC end-of-life March 2029

changes.summary: Docker Pro +80% ($5→$9/mo), Team +67% ($9→$15/mo). Free tier: Build Cloud minutes removed, Scout repos cut 3→1, private repos limited to 1 with 2GB storage
changes.previous_state: Pro $5/user/month, Team $9/user/month. Free tier included Build Cloud minutes, 3 Scout repos
changes.current_state: Pro $9/user/month, Team $15/user/month. Free: 1 private repo (2GB), 1 Scout repo, no Build Cloud minutes, 40 pulls/hour

changes.summary: Hatch startup program expanded to up to $100K compute credits plus up to 3 months free H100 GPU access for AI/ML startups
changes.previous_state: Standard Hatch startup credits program
changes.current_state: Up to $100K compute credits (12 months, excludes GPU). Separate GPU benefit: discounted H100 at ~$1.90/hr (vs $3.39 standard) plus up to 3 months free GPU access

changes.summary: Google for Startups Cloud Program now offers up to $350K GCP credits for AI-first startups, plus $10K Anthropic (Claude) partner credits via Model Garden
changes.previous_state: Up to $200K GCP credits for standard startups
changes.current_state: Scale AI Tier: up to $350K GCP credits ($250K year 1, $100K year 2). Standard: $200K. Partner credits: $10K Anthropic/Claude via Model Garden, Fireworks AI, MongoDB Atlas, CockroachDB, 1yr free GitLab Ultimate

changes.summary: EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML GPU prices increased ~15% across regions. p5e.48xlarge went from $34.61 to $39.80/hr (US East) and $43.26 to $49.75/hr (US West N. California)
changes.previous_state: p5e.48xlarge at $34.61/hr (US East), $43.26/hr (US West N. California) for Capacity Blocks
changes.current_state: p5e.48xlarge at $39.80/hr (US East), $49.75/hr (US West N. California). ~15% increase across most GPU Capacity Block instances

changes.summary: Google Developer Program Premium merged into Google One AI Pro ($19.99/mo, includes $10 Cloud credits) and AI Ultra ($100 Cloud credits). Developer benefits now bundled into consumer AI subscriptions
changes.previous_state: Separate Developer Program Premium subscription ($299/year) with dev tools and perks
changes.current_state: Developer benefits folded into AI Pro ($19.99/mo, $10 Cloud credits for Vertex AI/Cloud Run/Gemini API) and AI Ultra ($100 Cloud credits). Developer Program Premium being phased out for @gmail.com accounts

changes.summary: Assistants API deprecated, full shutdown August 26, 2026. Developers must migrate to Responses API + Conversations API
changes.previous_state: Assistants API available with code interpreter, file search, function calling, and Threads for conversation management
changes.current_state: Deprecated. Full shutdown Aug 26, 2026. Migration required: Assistants → Prompts + Responses API, Threads → Conversations API. No automated migration tool provided

changes.summary: Cloud & dedicated server prices increasing 30-50%. Entry-level CX23: €2.99→€3.99/mo (+33%). Applies to new and existing customers. Driven by DRAM (+171% YoY) and NVMe SSD cost surges from AI infrastructure demand
changes.previous_state: Lower cloud server pricing across Germany, Finland, US, and Singapore data centers (e.g., CX23 at €2.99/mo)
changes.current_state: 30-37% increase on cloud servers, up to 50% on select dedicated/storage products. All regions and all customers (new and existing) affected. Announced Feb 23, 2026

changes.summary: Claude Opus 4.6 API pricing at $5/$25 per MTok (input/output) — 67% below previous Opus 4/4.1 pricing of $15/$75. Frontier AI model at mid-tier prices
changes.previous_state: Opus 4/4.1 priced at $15/$75 per million tokens (input/output)
changes.current_state: Opus 4.6 at $5/$25 per MTok. Extended context (>200K tokens): $10/$37.50. Batch API: $2.50/$12.50. Price reduction began with Opus 4.5 generation

changes.summary: Ads launched in ChatGPT Free and Go ($8/mo) tiers. Sponsored units from major brands appear below responses on first prompt. $60 CPM, $200K minimum ad commitment
changes.previous_state: Ad-free experience across all ChatGPT tiers including Free
changes.current_state: Free and Go tiers show contextual ads (labeled 'sponsored') below responses. Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise/Education tiers remain ad-free. Users can opt out at cost of reduced daily message limits

changes.summary: Free full-web search being discontinued. Free tier restricted to max 50 domains per search engine. Migration deadline January 1, 2027
changes.previous_state: Free search across the entire web with 10,000 queries/day limit via Custom Search JSON API
changes.current_state: Free tier restricted to searching up to 50 specified domains only. Full-web search requires paid Google Cloud Search or Vertex AI Search. Takes effect January 1, 2027

changes.summary: odrive free tier discontinued effective March 31, 2026. Previously offered free unified cloud storage sync across multiple providers (Google Drive, Dropbox, S3, etc.)
changes.previous_state: Free tier with basic sync across multiple cloud storage providers, file manager interface
changes.current_state: Free tier removed. Business plans at $20/user/month (min 5 users) or $200/year org subscription. March 31, 2026 deadline

changes.summary: MinIO archived its open-source GitHub repository. All development moved to proprietary MinIO AIStor. No new Docker images, PRs, or contributions accepted. Only case-by-case critical security fixes
changes.previous_state: Open-source S3-compatible object storage under AGPL-3.0. Active GitHub repo with community contributions, regular Docker image releases
changes.current_state: GitHub repository archived. Development moved to proprietary AIStor product. No new open-source releases. Critical security fixes only on case-by-case basis

changes.summary: Per-user monthly Azure credits eliminated, replaced with organization-level bulk Azure credits. Visual Studio license in all benefit tiers now exclusively VS Enterprise IDE
changes.previous_state: Per-user monthly Azure credits allocated individually. Mix of Visual Studio Professional and Enterprise licenses across tiers
changes.current_state: Organization-level bulk Azure credits (shared pool). All tiers include VS Enterprise IDE only. Per-user monthly credits discontinued

changes.summary: Startup program revamped to 4 tiers with up to $250,000 in credits (previously lower). Tiers: Bootstrapped $5K, Up-and-Coming $25K, Seed-Funded $100K, High Growth $250K. Credits expire within 1 year
changes.previous_state: Smaller startup program with fewer tiers and lower credit amounts
changes.current_state: 4 tiers: $5K, $25K, $100K, $250K credits. Covers Workers, R2, Workers AI, Stream. Caps: $10K for R2/Cache Reserve, $50K for Workers AI. Credits expire within 1 year

changes.summary: Gemini 2.0 Flash and 2.0 Flash-Lite models deprecated, scheduled for retirement. Free tier continues with Gemini 2.5 models. Developers on 2.0 must migrate
changes.previous_state: Gemini 2.0 Flash and Flash-Lite available as free-tier models with standard rate limits
changes.current_state: 2.0 Flash and Flash-Lite deprecated. Developers must migrate to Gemini 2.5 Flash or 2.5 Pro. Free tier preserved for 2.5 series

changes.summary: Free Community plan (1 GB/day, 1-day retention, 10 alerts) removed. Only 14-day free trial remains. Consumption-based pricing starts at $0.92/ingested GB/day
changes.previous_state: Free Community plan: 1 GB/day ingestion, 1-day retention, 10 alerts, ML-powered anomaly detection
changes.current_state: 14-day free trial only. Pay-as-go: $0.92/GB/day logs, $0.40/1K metrics/day, $0.16/1M spans/day. No permanent free tier

changes.summary: Pay-per-use pricing model introduced alongside existing fixed tiers. Legacy free tier users migrated to pay-per-use with $10 voucher. Free tier effectively reduced to proof-of-concept only (~500 posts/month read, no search)
changes.previous_state: Fixed tiers: Free (limited), Basic $100/mo, Pro $5,000/mo, Enterprise $42,000/mo
changes.current_state: Pay-per-use model added: per-operation billing (read, search, write priced separately). Fixed tiers remain: Free (very limited), Basic $200/mo, Pro $5,000/mo, Enterprise $42,000/mo

changes.summary: Free trial credits ($5-$18 for new accounts) completely discontinued. Free tier now limited to GPT-3.5 Turbo at 3 RPM with no credits. All advanced models (GPT-4, DALL-E, Whisper) require paid access
changes.previous_state: $5-$18 in free trial credits for new accounts, 3-month expiry, access to all models
changes.current_state: No free credits. Free tier: GPT-3.5 Turbo only, 3 requests/minute. Must add payment method for any other model access

changes.summary: Xero retiring revenue-share model, moving to 5-tier connection+egress pricing. No more free API access for developers. Costs jumping from ~$0 to $17K+/year for some. Also banning API data use for AI/ML model training
changes.previous_state: Revenue-share model with effective free API access for developers building Xero integrations
changes.current_state: 5-tier usage-based pricing: connection fees + data egress charges. No free tier. Explicit prohibition on using API data for AI/ML training

changes.summary: Perpetual free tier (100 emails/day) eliminated. Replaced with 60-day trial only. Paid plans start at $19.95/month
changes.previous_state: Free plan: 100 emails/day forever, no credit card required
changes.current_state: 60-day trial only (100 emails/day, 100 contacts). Paid plans from $19.95/month after trial

changes.summary: Cloud Storage removed from Spark (free) plan. Projects on Spark with default appspot.com buckets lose console access and API calls return 402/403 errors. Must upgrade to Blaze (pay-as-you-go) to use Cloud Storage
changes.previous_state: Spark plan included Cloud Storage: 5 GB storage, 1 GB/day download, 20K uploads/day, 50K downloads/day
changes.current_state: Cloud Storage requires Blaze plan. Blaze provides no-cost quota (1 GiB storage, 10 GB download/month) before charges begin, but credit card required. Other Spark limits unchanged (Firestore, Auth, Functions, Hosting)

changes.summary: PlanetScale removed free Hobby plan entirely. All databases on free tier deleted after 30-day grace period. Minimum plan now Scaler at $39/month
changes.previous_state: Free Hobby plan: 5 GB storage, 1B row reads/month, 10M row writes/month, 1 production branch, 1 development branch
changes.current_state: No free tier. Scaler plan starts at $39/month (25 GB storage). Enterprise custom pricing. Company pivoted to enterprise focus

changes.summary: Fauna shut down entirely on May 30, 2025. The serverless document database ceased all operations. Over 80,000 development teams affected
changes.previous_state: Free tier: 100K read ops/day, 50K write ops/day, 500K compute ops/day, 5 GB storage. Consumption-based pricing above free limits
changes.current_state: Service discontinued. No longer available in any form. All customer data deleted

changes.summary: LocalStack drops open-source Community Edition. Single unified image requires auth token. Free tier replaced with non-commercial-only tier requiring registration. Commercial use requires paid plan starting at $39/month
changes.previous_state: Open-source Community Edition: 30+ emulated AWS services (S3, Lambda, DynamoDB, etc.), no registration required, Docker image freely available
changes.current_state: Non-commercial free tier requires registration and auth token. Commercial use starts at $39/month. CI credits system introduced. OSS projects, students, and nonprofits get free access

changes.summary: Google Developer Program annual subscription ending March 30. Replaced by Google AI Pro ($10/mo) and AI Ultra ($100/mo) tiers with bundled cloud credits and AI model access.
changes.previous_state: Google Developer Program: annual subscription with standalone cloud credits, developer tools access, and learning resources
changes.current_state: Google AI Pro ($10/mo) and AI Ultra ($100/mo) tiers bundle cloud credits with AI model access (Gemini, etc). Developer benefits folded into AI-centric subscriptions

changes.summary: Gruntwork launches free tier for Terragrunt Scale IaC orchestration platform, positioned as HCP Terraform free tier alternative ahead of HashiCorp March 31, 2026 EOL. Includes GitOps, drift detection, and module update automation
changes.previous_state: No free tier — Team plan at $500/month
changes.current_state: Free tier with limits matching or exceeding HCP Terraform (500+ managed resources, GitOps, drift detection, Patcher)

changes.summary: HashiCorp legacy free plan for HCP Terraform ends March 31, 2026. All legacy free plan users auto-migrated to enhanced free tier with different limits: 500 managed resources (previously unlimited for small teams), unlimited users, 1 concurrent run, SSO, and policy as code (Sentinel + OPA)
changes.previous_state: Legacy free plan with basic Terraform Cloud features
changes.current_state: Enhanced free tier: 500 managed resources, unlimited users, 1 concurrent run, SSO, policy as code (Sentinel + OPA). Auto-migration from legacy plan

changes.summary: X/Twitter API eliminated free tier in February 2026, replacing it with a pay-per-use credit model. Active free-tier users receive a $0 voucher. 'For-good' utility apps remain free. Basic fixed tier remains at $200/month
changes.previous_state: Free tier: ~500 posts/month read, 1,500 tweets/month write, no search, ~1 request per 15 minutes
changes.current_state: No free tier. Pay-per-use credit model replaces free access. $0 voucher for existing free-tier users. For-good utility apps exempted

changes.summary: Launched $20/month Core plan with pay-as-you-go ACU pricing, down from previous $500/month-only Team plan
changes.previous_state: Team plan only at $500/month (250 credits)
changes.current_state: Core plan $20/month (pay-as-you-go at $2.25/ACU), Team plan $500/month remains

changes.summary: Moved from flat monthly subscription to credit-based pricing model with new $200/month Ultra tier
changes.previous_state: Pro plan $20/month with 500 fast requests
changes.current_state: Pro plan $20/month (credit-based), Business $40/seat/month, Ultra $200/month (higher credit limits)

changes.summary: Shifted from per-seat subscription to credit-based consumption model
changes.previous_state: Per-seat monthly subscription
changes.current_state: Credit-based consumption model with usage tiers

changes.summary: Google Tenor public API shutting down June 30, 2026. New API key signups halted January 13, 2026. Only the public GIF search API is affected — Tenor website and app continue operating. Developers must migrate to alternative GIF APIs
changes.previous_state: Free public API for GIF search, trending GIFs, and autocomplete. No rate limits for reasonable use. Used by messaging apps and social platforms
changes.current_state: API shutdown June 30, 2026. No new API keys issued since Jan 13, 2026. Existing keys work until shutdown date. No replacement API from Google

changes.summary: Docker acquired AtomicJar (creators of Testcontainers Cloud). Standalone Testcontainers Cloud free tier retired — now bundled into Docker subscriptions. Docker Pro gets 100 min/month, Team 500 min/month, Business 1,500 min/month
changes.previous_state: Standalone Testcontainers Cloud with independent free tier (100 min/month)
changes.current_state: Bundled with Docker subscriptions. No standalone free tier. Open-source Testcontainers libraries remain MIT-licensed and fully free

changes.summary: Docker Desktop subscription prices increased 67-80% across all tiers. Personal $7→$11, Pro $9→$14, Team $15→$24, Business $24→$35 per user/month. Separate from Docker Hub registry pricing changes
changes.previous_state: Personal $7/user/month, Pro $9/user/month, Team $15/user/month, Business $24/user/month
changes.current_state: Personal $11/user/month (+57%), Pro $14/user/month (+56%), Team $24/user/month (+60%), Business $35/user/month (+46%). All tiers affected

changes.summary: Free tier significantly expanded: storage 5x increase to 25 GB, 100 GB egress added (new), per-seat charges removed. Unity DevOps (formerly Plastic SCM) now offers generous free version control for game developers
changes.previous_state: 5 GB storage, no egress allowance, per-seat pricing for teams
changes.current_state: 25 GB storage (5x increase), 100 GB egress/month (new), per-seat charges removed. Free for individuals and small teams

changes.summary: Temporary usage promotion: double five-hour usage limits during off-peak hours, March 13-27, 2026. Applies to Claude Pro and Team subscribers
changes.previous_state: Standard five-hour usage limits for Pro and Team subscribers
changes.current_state: 2x usage limits during off-peak hours (March 13-27, 2026 only). Temporary promotion to showcase increased capacity

changes.summary: Heroku removed all free dynos, free Heroku Postgres, and free Heroku Data for Redis. The iconic PaaS that defined 'free tier' for a generation eliminated free offerings entirely after Salesforce acquisition
changes.previous_state: Free dyno: 550 hours/month (1,000 with credit card), free Postgres: 10K rows, free Redis: 25 MB. Dynos slept after 30 min inactivity
changes.current_state: No free tier. Mini plan starts at $5/month (Eco dynos $5/month for 1,000 shared hours). Postgres starts at $5/month

changes.summary: GitHub launched Copilot Free tier — AI code completion available to all GitHub users at no cost. 2,000 completions and 50 chat messages per month included
changes.previous_state: Paid only: Individual $10/month, Business $19/user/month. Free for verified students, teachers, and OSS maintainers only
changes.current_state: Free tier: 2,000 code completions/month, 50 chat messages/month. Individual $10/month, Business $19/month, Enterprise $39/month remain for higher limits

changes.summary: Auth0 increased free tier from 7,500 to 25,000 monthly active users. Largest free tier expansion since Okta acquisition. Makes Auth0 competitive with Clerk and Firebase Auth on free tier
changes.previous_state: Free tier: 7,500 MAU, unlimited social connections, 2 organizations
changes.current_state: Free tier: 25,000 MAU (+233%), unlimited social connections, 5 organizations. Actions, Branding, MFA included

changes.summary: Railway expanded free tier following $100M Series B. Trial plan now includes $5 in free credits/month, sufficient for small hobby projects. Removed credit card requirement for trial
changes.previous_state: $5 free trial credit one-time, credit card required for Hobby plan
changes.current_state: Trial: $5/month in free credits, no credit card needed. Hobby: $5/month + usage. Pro: $20/month per seat

changes.summary: Free web services now spin down after 15 minutes of inactivity (previously 30 minutes). Cold starts take 30-60 seconds. Free tier remains but with faster sleep times
changes.previous_state: Free web services slept after 30 minutes of inactivity
changes.current_state: Free web services sleep after 15 minutes of inactivity. 750 hours/month free compute unchanged. Cold start 30-60 seconds

changes.summary: Redis switched from BSD license to dual SSPL/RSALv2 license, effectively ending open-source status. Triggered creation of Valkey fork by Linux Foundation. Redis Cloud pricing unchanged but community trust impacted
changes.previous_state: BSD 3-Clause open-source license. Community-driven development. Multiple hosted providers (AWS ElastiCache, etc.)
changes.current_state: Dual SSPL + RSALv2 license. Cloud hosting by third parties restricted. Valkey fork (BSD) created by Linux Foundation with AWS, Google, Oracle backing

changes.summary: HashiCorp switched Terraform, Vault, Consul, Nomad, and other products from MPL 2.0 to BSL 1.1 license. Triggered OpenTofu fork under Linux Foundation. IBM acquired HashiCorp for $6.4B in 2024
changes.previous_state: Mozilla Public License 2.0 (open-source). Community-driven development with corporate stewardship
changes.current_state: Business Source License 1.1. Non-production use free, competitive commercial use restricted. OpenTofu fork (MPL 2.0) maintained by Linux Foundation

changes.summary: 20% price cut on Basic Droplets (cheapest now $4/mo from $5/mo). Per-second billing introduced (minimum 60 seconds or $0.01). Functions free tier increased to 25,000 GiB-seconds/mo
changes.previous_state: Basic Droplets from $5/mo, hourly billing, Functions 90K GiB-seconds/mo free
changes.current_state: Basic Droplets from $4/mo (1 vCPU, 512 MB, 10 GB SSD). Per-second billing (min 60s/$0.01). Functions 25,000 GiB-seconds/mo free

changes.summary: Managed databases are not part of the free tier. Pricing starts at $15/month. Previous listing incorrectly included '1 basic cluster' as a free tier benefit
changes.previous_state: Listed as: Free static sites (up to 3), functions (90K GiB-seconds/mo), and managed databases (1 basic cluster)
changes.current_state: Free static sites (up to 3) and functions (90K GiB-seconds/mo). Managed databases start at $15/mo, not free

changes.summary: Freshping shut down entirely. Free accounts disabled March 6, 2026. Paid plans expire at end of term. All data deleted 90 days later (~June 4, 2026). No replacement product offered by Freshworks
changes.previous_state: Free plan: 50 monitors, 1-minute check intervals, public status pages, multi-location checks, email/Slack alerts
changes.current_state: Service discontinued. No free or paid plan available

changes.summary: Firebase Studio (formerly Project IDX) shut down March 19, 2026. Cloud IDE and AI code generation features discontinued. Existing projects accessible until March 2027 for migration
changes.previous_state: Firebase Studio: cloud IDE with AI code generation, Gemini integration, multi-platform preview
changes.current_state: Discontinued. Projects accessible until March 2027 for data export only. Google recommends migrating to other IDEs

changes.summary: Spark plan projects using legacy *.appspot.com Cloud Storage buckets forced to upgrade to Blaze (pay-as-you-go) plan or lose Cloud Storage access. No billing caps on Blaze plan — developers risk unexpected charges
changes.previous_state: Spark plan: free Cloud Storage with *.appspot.com buckets, no credit card required
changes.current_state: Legacy bucket projects must upgrade to Blaze (pay-as-you-go). No hard billing caps. New projects use Firebase Storage buckets on Spark plan

changes.summary: Free tier link limit reduced 40x from 1,000 links to 25 new links/month. Added 30-day analytics retention limit and 1-user cap
changes.previous_state: 1,000 links, 1,000 tracked clicks/month, up to 3 custom domains
changes.current_state: 25 new links/month, 1,000 tracked events/month, 3 custom domains, 1 user, 30-day analytics retention

changes.summary: Free tier eliminated. Uploadthing moved to usage-based pricing with minimum $25/month plan (250 GB storage). No free tier available
changes.previous_state: Free tier: 2 GB storage, 2 GB bandwidth/month
changes.current_state: Usage-based pricing only. Minimum $25/month plan with 250 GB storage. No free tier

changes.summary: Data correction — previous entry incorrectly reduced limits to 50K nodes/175K relationships. Actual free tier is 200,000 nodes and 400,000 relationships per Neo4j AuraDB FAQ
changes.previous_state: 50,000 nodes, 175,000 relationships (incorrectly listed)
changes.current_state: 200,000 nodes, 400,000 relationships (restored to correct values)

changes.summary: Grok Imagine free image/video generation locked behind SuperGrok subscription ($30/mo). Over 1B generations in first month overwhelmed capacity
changes.previous_state: Free image and video generation for all Grok users
changes.current_state: Image/video generation requires SuperGrok subscription ($30/month). Text-only Grok remains free with limits

changes.summary: Billing-account-level spend caps enforced starting April 1, 2026. When a tier's spend cap is reached, API requests pause until the next billing month. Affects pay-as-you-go developers who may see unexpected request pausing.
changes.previous_state: No hard spend caps — usage billed without automatic pausing
changes.current_state: Tier-level spend caps enforced at billing account level. Requests pause when cap is hit until next month

changes.summary: Windsurf replaced credits-based pricing with daily/weekly quotas (Mar 19, 2026). New tiers: Pro $20/mo (was $15), Ultimate $40/mo, Team $200/mo/seat. Free tier changed from flexible credits to hard quotas. Existing Pro subscribers grandfathered at $15/mo
changes.previous_state: Credits-based system allowing flexible allocation between completions and chat. Pro plan $15/month
changes.current_state: Daily/weekly quotas replace credits. Pro $20/mo (existing subs grandfathered at $15), Ultimate $40/mo, Team $200/mo/seat. Free tier gets limited daily completions and chat messages

changes.summary: Google launched Gemini Code Assist free tier for individual developers. Includes code completions, chat, and multi-file editing powered by Gemini 2.5 Pro. Available in VS Code, JetBrains, and Cloud Shell
changes.previous_state: No free tier — enterprise-only at $19/user/month
changes.current_state: Free individual tier with code completions, chat, and multi-file editing. Enterprise tier remains at $19/user/month

changes.summary: Aurora PostgreSQL Serverless added to AWS Free Tier — managed PostgreSQL-compatible database now available at no cost with up to 4 ACUs per cluster and 1 GB storage. Part of AWS Free Plan (6 months + credits)
changes.previous_state: No free tier — Aurora was paid-only, starting at ~$0.08/ACU-hour
changes.current_state: Free plan with up to 4 ACUs per cluster, 1 GB storage, $100–200 in credits for new accounts
